message:
🌱 Add vllm: Inquiry Regarding vLLM Support for Mac Metal API mission (#845)

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,79 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "vllm-2081-inquiry-regarding-vllm-support-for-mac-metal-api",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "vllm: Mac Metal API support",
+    "description": "Enable vLLM to run on Apple Silicon Macs using the Metal Performance Shaders (MPS) backend via PyTorch's MPS fallback mode. Requested by 48+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current vLLM setup",
+        "description": "Verify your vLLM version and Python environment:\n```bash\npip show vllm | grep Version\npython -c \"import torch; print(torch.backends.mps.is_available())\"\n```\nMac Metal support requires an Apple Silicon Mac (M1/M2/M3/M4) with PyTorch MPS backend available."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Install vLLM with MPS support",
+        "description": "Install or build vLLM from source with MPS support enabled:\n```bash\npip install vllm\n```\nThe MPS backend uses PyTorch's MPS fallback mode, where supported operators run natively on MPS and others fall back to CPU. This is experimental but functional for basic inference."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Start the vLLM server on Mac",
+        "description": "Launch the vLLM server with a small model suitable for Mac hardware:\n```bash\npython -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server \\\n  --model Qwen/Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct \\\n  --device mps\n```\nNote: Not all models are supported on MPS. Start with smaller models and check compatibility."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify Metal API inference works",
+        "description": "Test that inference is working on the MPS backend:\n```bash\ncurl http://localhost:8000/v1/completions \\\n  -H \"Content-Type: application/json\" \\\n  -d '{\"model\": \"Qwen/Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct\", \"prompt\": \"San Francisco is a\", \"max_tokens\": 7, \"temperature\": 0}'\n```\nYou should receive a valid completion response running on Apple Metal."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "vLLM added experimental Mac Metal API support using PyTorch's MPS fallback mode. This enables running vLLM on Apple Silicon Macs where supported PyTorch operators run natively on MPS while other operations fall back to CPU. While not as performant as CUDA, this allows local development and inference on Mac hardware.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "# Start vLLM on Mac with MPS backend\npython -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server --model Qwen/Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct --device mps",
+        "# Test inference\ncurl http://localhost:8000/v1/completions \\\n  -H \"Content-Type: application/json\" \\\n  -d '{\"model\": \"Qwen/Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct\", \"prompt\": \"San Francisco is a\", \"max_tokens\": 7, \"temperature\": 0}'"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "vllm",
+      "community",
+      "llm-serving",
+      "feature",
+      "apple-silicon",
+      "metal",
+      "mps"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "vllm"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "community",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/issues/2081",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm",
+      "pr": "https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/12640"
+    },
+    "reactions": 48,
+    "comments": 22,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "tools": [
+      "python",
+      "pip"
+    ],
+    "description": "Apple Silicon Mac (M1/M2/M3/M4), Python 3.8+, pip, PyTorch with MPS backend, and vLLM (pip install vllm)."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-03-12T06:44:54.950Z",
+    "scannerVersion": "cncf-gen-3.0.0",
+    "sanitized": true,
+    "findings": []
+  }
+}
